qa_pairs.question: What `tar` option is used to add files to an existing archive without having to create a new one?
qa_pairs.answer: The `-r` option is used to add files to an existing `tar` archive without creating a new one.

qa_pairs.question: How do you add the file `new.log.dat` to an existing archive named `results.tar` using the `tar` command?
qa_pairs.answer: You can add the file using the command: `tar -rf results.tar new.log.dat`.

qa_pairs.question: Can files be added to compressed `tar` archives (e.g., `*.gz` or `*.bz2`) directly?
qa_pairs.answer: No, files cannot be added directly to compressed archives (`*.gz` or `*.bz2`). They can only be added to plain `tar` archives (`*.tar`).

qa_pairs.question: Can the `-r` option in `tar` also be used to add a directory to an existing `tar` file?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, the `-r` option can also be used to append or add a directory or directories to an existing `tar` file.

qa_pairs.question: What command would you use to add the directory `report/` to an existing `tar` archive named `results.tar`?
qa_pairs.answer: You would use the command: `tar -rf results.tar report/`.

qa_pairs.question: Which `tar` option allows you to combine two archive files into a single one?
qa_pairs.answer: The `-A` option is used to combine two archive files into one.

qa_pairs.question: How would you add the archive `report.tar` to an existing archive `results.tar`?
qa_pairs.answer: You would use the command: `tar -A -f results.tar report.tar`.

qa_pairs.question: Is the `-v` option mandatory when listing the contents of a `tar` file after appending, if you don't want to see file details?
qa_pairs.answer: No, the `-v` option (for verbosity) is not necessary if you do not want to show the details of the files when listing the contents of a `tar` file.
